title: Sampacca euonymoides
intro:
There is no available information on "Sampacca euonymoides." However, I found information on a plant called "Sampaca" which is also known as "Sampaka" or "Sampacca guianensis." 

Sampaca (Sampaca guianensis) is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and treating various infections due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sampaca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. Sampaca is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, fever, and infections.
